rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND VINCENT DIGIACOMO JR. VS. RECOLOGY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Requiring Plaintiff To Furnish Security; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, March 28, 2025 Line 2. DEFENDANTs RECOLOGY, INC., GOLDEN GATE DISPOSAL AND RECYCLING COMPANY, RECOLOGY SAN FRANCISCO, INC.,and MARCUS MIRT's Motion For An Order Requiring Plaintiff To Furnish Security. Continued to April 14, 2025, and transferred to be heard in Department 301 per order of the presiding judge. Judge Quinn is challenged. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: JOSE MARIA DEL CID VS. CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Re Petition For Relief
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, March 28, 2025 Line 10. PLAINTIFF JOSE DEL CID Notice Of Hearing Re Petition For Relief. Petitioner's petition for relief from the government claims requirement under Government Code section 946.6 is denied. (See Dept. of Water & Power v. Superior Court (2000) 82 Cal.App.4th 1288, 1291 ["Relief from failure to timely present a government tort claim is available only if the applicant establishes by a preponderance of the evidence the failure was 'through mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect.'"].) First, no competent evidence supports the petition. Plaintiff's "affidavit" is not under penalty of perjury in compliance with CCP 2015.5. Second, plaintiff fails to demonstrate mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect. Plaintiff asserts that it took him time to find a mechanic. But the record shows he obtained a mechanic's estimate on December 27, 2023, well within the 6-month time period. (Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice ("RJN") C.) Plaintiff claims that there was a language barrier, but his "affidavit" does not mention this issue, is in clear English, and there is no evidence that it was originally provided in another language or translated before execution. In any event, defendant provides instructions to claimants in Spanish. (Defendant's RJN, A, B.) Lastly, plaintiff's claimed lack of knowledge of how to obtain legal support is unexplained and unconvincing. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH ALEN BANKS JR VS. BART POLICE DEPARTMENT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice & Motion For Discovery Request For Police Report(S) & Evidence Collected In Report 2309-0720
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, March 28, 2025 Line 13. 2 - PLAINTIFF JOSEPH BANKS' Motion For Discovery Request For Police Report(S) & Evidence Collected In Report 2309-0720. Plaintiff Joseph Alen Banks, Jr's Motion For Discovery Request For Police Report(S) & Evidence Collected In Report 2309-0720 against Defendant Officers N. Marson, J. Miller and J. Senghvilay is DENIED. The court construes Plaintiff's motion as a discovery motion to compel. Discovery in this court is governed by the Civil Discovery Act. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 2016 et seq.) The California Civil Discovery Act sets out the rules and methods for a party to obtain material information from another. Those rules include several steps that must be taken before a party may seek an order compelling another to provide information. Here, the record does not show that Plaintiff has taken any of those steps. Thus, the court denies the motion. Plaintiff is advised to review and follow the Civil Discovery Act and associated rules. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
